name: Dr Benjamin McKeeby
focus: Thermal infrared spectroscopy • Planetary surfaces • Lunar and Martian geology
bio: Benjamin E. McKeeby is a planetary geologist and remote sensing specialist at Northern Arizona University, where he serves as a postdoctoral researcher in the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). His research focuses on understanding the composition, physical properties, and evolution of planetary surfaces, particularly on Mars and the Moon. Dr. McKeeby uses thermal infrared spectroscopy, imaging data, and thermophysical modeling to analyze surface processes and interpret geological features across planetary bodies. His work also includes studying planetary analog environments on Earth and investigating lunar volatiles to better understand surface-atmosphere interactions. Through integrating spacecraft observations, field studies, and laboratory techniques, he contributes to advancing knowledge of planetary surface dynamics and the processes that shape extraterrestrial landscapes.
